channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Implementing a JSON Schema Validator from Scratch - Week 7
item.description: As I stated last time, this was a pretty short week. I only added one thing to the validator, which is evaluation phases. The idea is simple, not all keywords are created equal, some need to be...

item.title: I Built a Blockchain Voting System with RSA Encryption — Here's How It Works
item.description: Digital voting is one of those problems that sounds simple until you actually think about it. How do you prove a vote was counted? How do you stop someone from voting twice? How do you make...

item.title: What a €20 AI audit actually looks like (full example inside)
item.description: Before paying for anything, you should be able to judge the work. So here's a complete audit I ran on a real product — Balsamiq, the wireframing tool — unedited, so you can decide if this is worth...

item.title: AI Execution Hallucination: When Your Agent Says "Done" and Does Nothing
item.description: I run Jarvis, a Claude based AI operations system that handles my email, content pipeline, reminders, and daily briefings via Telegram and a stack of Python scripts. For a while, I had a bug I...

item.title: Marathon is in a sprint
item.description: Marathon is weird as hell - and I mean that in the best possible way. The latest game from Destiny studio Bungie mixes the slick gunplay the developer is known for with a dark, and frankly...

item.description: Whether you need to monitor website changes, generate social previews, create PDF reports, or archive web content, taking screenshots programmatically is a common Node.js task. Here's every...

item.title: Meet your new phone away from phone
item.description: Unihertz's booth at MWC was a little out of the way, but those who did find it all seemed to want to pick up the Titan Elite 2. Sure, the cosmic orange color attracted attention, and the QWERTY...

item.title: Claude Used to Hack Mexican Government
item.description: An unknown hacker used Anthropic’s LLM to hack the Mexican government: The unknown Claude user wrote Spanish-language prompts for the chatbot to act as an elite hacker, finding vulnerabilities in...

item.title: Why Is Alexa+ So Bad?
item.description: I stuck Amazon’s Echo Show 15 and its Alexa+ AI assistant in my kitchen for a month. Things have not gone well.

item.title: The MSP Guide to Using AI-Powered Risk Management to Scale Cybersecurity
item.description: Scaling cybersecurity services as an MSP or MSSP requires technical expertise and a business model that delivers measurable value at scale. Risk-based cybersecurity is the foundation of that...

item.title: Iran-Linked MuddyWater Hackers Target U.S. Networks With New Dindoor Backdoor
item.description: New research from Broadcom's Symantec and Carbon Black Threat Hunter Team has discovered evidence of an Iranian hacking group embedding itself in several U.S. companies' networks, including banks,...

item.title: Jack Dorsey Is Ready to Explain the Block Layoffs
item.description: In an exclusive interview with WIRED, Block’s cofounder and CEO says he axed 40 percent of his workforce so that he can rebuild the company “as an intelligence.”

item.title: China-Linked Hackers Use TernDoor, PeerTime, BruteEntry in South American Telecom Attacks
item.description: A China-linked advanced persistent threat (APT) actor has been targeting critical telecommunications infrastructure in South America since 2024, targeting Windows and Linux systems and edge...

item.title: Microsoft Reveals ClickFix Campaign Using Windows Terminal to Deploy Lumma Stealer
item.description: Microsoft on Thursday disclosed details of a new widespread ClickFix social engineering campaign that has leveraged the Windows Terminal app as a way to activate a sophisticated attack chain and...

item.title: Hikvision and Rockwell Automation CVSS 9.8 Flaws Added to CISA KEV Catalog
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added two security flaws impacting Hikvision and Rockwell Automation products to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities...

item.title: Anthropic to challenge DOD’s supply-chain label in court
item.description: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei said he plans to challenge the Department of Defense's designation of the AI firm as a supply-chain risk. He claims most Anthropic customers are unaffected by the label.

item.title: Amazon.com says things are fixed after some issues with logging in and checking out
item.description: If you were having issues shopping on Amazon or loading your playlists on Amazon Music on Thursday, you weren't alone. For over three hours today, Downdetector showed a sizable spike in people...

item.title: The best iPad deals you can get right now
item.description: While the best iPad deals usually land during major sale events like Black Friday, many great iPad deals are available outside of those times. The day-to-day discounts come and go like changing...

item.title: What if your real computer was a supersized Lego computer brick?
item.description: In 1979 - nearly 50 years ago - Lego jazzed up its very first spaceships with an iconic sloped computer brick that was just painted plastic. In 2022, we introduced you to the engineer who fit an...

item.title: Lawmakers just advanced online safety laws that require age verification at the app store
item.description: A package of child safety bills is headed to the House floor following an hours-long session that left Democrats and Republicans divided. On Thursday, lawmakers on the House Committee on Energy...

item.title: DiligenceSquared uses AI, voice agents to make M&A research affordable
item.description: Instead of relying on expensive management consultants, the startup uses AI voice agents to conduct interviews with customers of the companies the PE firms are considering buying.

item.title: The Pentagon formally labels Anthropic a supply-chain risk
item.description: After weeks of failed negotiations, public ultimatums, and lawsuit threats, the Defense Department has formally labeled Anthropic a "supply-chain risk," escalating its fight with the AI company...

item.title: ‘Uncanny Valley’: Iran War in the AI Era, Prediction Market Ethics, and Paramount Beats Netflix
item.description: In this episode, our hosts unpack the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, particularly as the AI industry has been entrenching itself with the Department of Defense.

item.title: OpenAI Had Banned Military Use. The Pentagon Tested Its Models Through Microsoft Anyway
item.description: Sources allege the Defense Department experimented with Microsoft’s version of OpenAI technology before the ChatGPT-maker lifted its prohibition on military applications.

item.title: AWS launches a new AI agent platform specifically for healthcare
item.description: AWS is launching Amazon Connect Health, an AI agent platform that will help with patient scheduling, documentation, and patient verification.
